governing_requirement: Publish only installed and independently qualified provider routes, with exact model/workflow/runtime/hardware/benchmark/certificate identities, fresh contextual evidence, append-only performance history, champion/challenger/rollback state, and no self-promotion by observations.
prerequisites: MF-P6-10.01 remains open; a capability decision must bind an adopted release-publication evidence record and actual catalog bytes.
existing_enforcement: The frozen capability snapshot has a strict canonical top-level hash, fixture/runtime evidence firewall, typed access modes/endpoints, labels/artifacts/transforms/limits, authority policy and conditional native_venv/container runtime shape.
Provider stacks declare model, workflow, runtime, hardware, route, performance, champion, qualification, lifecycle and certificate identities.

Validation checks unique stack/hash/route IDs, promoted-route certificate presence and scope expiry, inactive certificate removal, complete mode eligibility, route/lifecycle/health consistency, availability freshness and live endpoint advertisement.
A partial provider list is schema-valid, allowing an honest partial model library.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Change a model artifact SHA-256 while retaining the old stack_sha256, then recompute only snapshot_sha256.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: No stack self-hash or installed artifact-byte resolver proves the declared model/workflow/runtime/hardware route actually exists.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Replace promoted stack and eligibility certificate IDs with an invented nonempty ID and recompute snapshot_sha256.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: Certificate presence and date shape are checked, but no signed benchmark certificate bytes, scope, signer, revocation or route binding is supplied.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Move performance_profile.observed_at to 2000-01-01 while leaving current availability and recompute snapshot_sha256.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: Performance evidence has no freshness ceiling, source/correlation identity, append-only ledger head or independent-evidence requirement.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Replace champion ID/hash with invented values and recompute snapshot_sha256.
confirmed_gaps.interpretation: Every stack carries status=current, but no closed champion/challenger arbitration, previous champion, promotion transaction, rollback route or tested rollback evidence is validated.

confirmed_gaps.observed: Lifecycle values exist, but the snapshot has no append-only contextual performance ledger or deterministic rule preventing newly installed/self-reported/correlated evidence from entering directly as promoted.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement an additive producer capability qualification/publication decision over the frozen snapshot that resolves actual installed bytes, independent benchmark/certificate evidence, contextual ledger history, and champion/challenger/rollback state.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate paths are read-only; src/maskfactory/validation.py remains dirty in preserved 08.05 scope and is excluded from mutation. Allowed paths are disjoint from held 09.01-09.07, 10.01 and 10.03 units.
validator_commands: python -m pytest -q tests/test_bridge_capability_snapshot.py tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'capability or adoption'
acceptance_criteria: Emit a strict canonical self-hashed capability decision binding the exact release-publication evidence, frozen snapshot hash, catalog, policy, ledger checkpoint, decision time and current revocation head.

Resolve model/workflow/runtime/hardware/route/performance/certificate identities to actual cataloged bytes and recompute every stack hash; absent, changed, unqualified or unresolved bytes cannot be eligible.

Validate signed benchmark certificates by trusted role, exact route/label/context/artifact/person/dimension scope, current validity and revocation; IDs or self-reported hashes alone grant no eligibility.
Use an append-only contextual performance ledger with source/run/dataset/split/seed/hardware/runtime/metric identities, independence/correlation classification, freshness and canonical head hash.
Newly installed providers enter as draft/challenger only. Promotion requires closed non-inferiority/quality/resource/safety gates and an atomic signed transaction preserving the prior champion.
Publish exactly one compatible champion per route scope, explicit challengers and one tested rollback binding; close/ambiguous routes branch within budget or abstain, never self-promote.
Derive mode availability/eligibility from current qualified routes, certificates and health evidence; route and certificate IDs must resolve exactly and reason codes are closed/deterministic.
A partial installed library remains a valid honest snapshot, while missing providers are absent rather than represented as eligible placeholders.

Negative tests cover stale stack hash, absent/changed bytes, invented/revoked/out-of-scope certificate, stale/correlated/self-reported metrics, direct newcomer promotion, dual champion, rollback drift and expired health.
Frozen v1 schemas, fixtures, validation.py and held worker paths remain byte-identical. Actual model ingestion remains deferred until Kevin confirms downloads complete.

observed_verification.command: four recomputed-snapshot probes: stale stack hash after model change, invented certificate, stale performance, invented champion
python -m pytest -q tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'positive_documents or production_qualification_and_adoption or fixture_adoption_replay'

claim_boundary: This audit isolates why a hash-valid capability declaration is not yet qualified production authority. It does not complete MF-P6-10.02, promote a provider, validate downloaded models, publish a release, or claim runtime/Main adoption.
